corrections.reason: The paper does not quantify 'content' as a trait metric for brucine in relation to SnvOMT. It shows enzymatic activity and product formation but not quantitative content measurements.

Gene_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names this gene 'Snv11H' in the text: 'One of these cytochrome P450 proteins (cluster 4032.16581; CYP71AH44, Snv11H)'

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Data Availability section lists 'Snv11H (OM304298)' as a GenBank accession number.

Species.reason: Snv11H is from S. nux-vomica, as indicated by the 'Snv' prefix and the context of discovery from the S. nux-vomica transcriptome.

Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper identifies the species as Strychnos nux-vomica throughout.

Gene_Role_Class.reason: Snv11H is a cytochrome P450 enzyme that catalyzes hydroxylation, clearly an enzyme role.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly states 'One of these cytochrome P450 proteins (cluster 4032.16581; CYP71AH44, Snv11H)', confirming both the cytochrome P450 family and the CYP71AH44 designation.

Enzyme_Name_or_Function.reason: The paper does not explicitly name Snv11H as 'strychnine-11-hydroxylase'. The paper shows it hydroxylates β-colubrine at the 11 position, but no formal enzyme name is given beyond 'Snv11H'. The name 'Strychnine-11-hydroxylase' is a reasonable inference but is not stated in the paper.

Final_Nutrient_Product.reason: Snv11H is part of the pathway that produces brucine 15. The paper demonstrates that Snv11H combined with SnvOMT produces brucine from β-colubrine.

Final_Nutrient_Product_Class.reason: Brucine is described as a monoterpene indole alkaloid throughout the paper. 'Alkaloid' is correct.

Nutrient_Trait_Metric.reason: The paper measures LC-MS peak areas of products, which relates to content/accumulation. However, 'content' as a specific metric is not explicitly stated; the paper reports LC-MS peak area data.

Pathway_Name.reason: The paper describes strychnine and brucine as monoterpene indole alkaloids. The pathway is explicitly described as monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis: 'Strychnine—a complex monoterpene indole alkaloid'.

Metabolic_Step_Type.reason: The paper never describes Snv11H as a rate-limiting step. There is no discussion of rate limitation for this enzyme. This appears to be an unsupported assertion.

Reaction_Substrate.reason: The paper explicitly states: 'When we infiltrated synthetic β-colubrine 13 into tobacco leaves that express Snv11H alone only 11-deMe brucine 14 is formed', confirming β-colubrine as the substrate.

Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output.reason: The paper states Snv11H converts β-colubrine 13 to 11-deMe brucine 14: 'When we infiltrated synthetic β-colubrine 13 into tobacco leaves that express Snv11H alone only 11-deMe brucine 14 is formed'.

Key_Intermediate_Metabolites_Affected.reason: 11-deMe brucine 14 is the intermediate produced by Snv11H from β-colubrine, as explicitly shown in the paper and Extended Data Fig. 9.

Flux_or_Direction_Change.reason: The paper confirms Snv11H hydroxylates β-colubrine at position 11 to form 11-deMe brucine, and SnvOMT then methylates to form brucine. This description accurately reflects the paper's findings.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient.reason: The paper shows Snv11H performs the hydroxylation of β-colubrine to 11-deMe brucine, which is the penultimate step before methylation to brucine. Calling it the 'final hydroxylation step in brucine biosynthesis' is accurate.

Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation.reason: The paper states brucine 'is also highly accumulated in the roots of S. nux-vomica' and that strychnos alkaloids 'accumulate in the roots'.

Expression_Pattern.reason: The paper states that it selected 13 cytochrome P450 proteins 'that were strongly expressed (FPKM ≥ 20) in roots' and Snv11H was among 3 within the CYP71 clade from this set. So FPKM ≥ 20 is implied for Snv11H, but not directly stated for Snv11H specifically. It is a reasonable inference.

Experimental_Materials.reason: The paper describes transient expression in N. benthamiana with β-colubrine infiltration (Extended Data Fig. 9c), and combinatorial expression with strychnine/Snv10H/SnvOMT (Extended Data Fig. 9b).

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper describes transient expression of Snv11H with β-colubrine substrate and LC-MS detection of 11-deMe brucine (Extended Data Fig. 9c), and combinatorial expression yielding brucine (Extended Data Fig. 9b,d).

Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method.reason: LC-MS (liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry) is explicitly used throughout the paper for product detection and quantification.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper states 'We generated tissue-specific RNA-sequencing data from these two plants to enable gene discovery', confirming RNA-seq transcriptomes were used.

Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method.reason: The paper states: 'the high accumulation of the final product brucine 15 in roots led us to identify all 13 other cytochrome P450 proteins that were strongly expressed (FPKM ≥ 20) in roots...we initially targeted the 3 within the CYP71 clade.' This matches the extraction.

Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value.reason: The paper concludes with discussion of metabolic engineering: 'these results highlight the promise for production of strychnos-type alkaloids using synthetic biology approaches' and mentions 'heterologous production of strychnos alkaloid derivatives'.

Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are supported: (1) Snv11H (CYP71AH44) hydroxylates β-colubrine to 11-deMe brucine - confirmed in Extended Data Fig. 9; (2) Expression with β-colubrine alone yields 11-deMe brucine, with SnvOMT yields brucine - explicitly stated; (3) Final hydroxylation step in brucine pathway - accurate based on the pathway.

Notes_or_Other_Important_Info.reason: The first part ('One of 3 cytochrome P450s within CYP71 clade tested') is supported. However, the second statement ('does not hydroxylate β-colubrine without SnvOMT present for complete conversion to brucine') is misleading. The paper clearly states Snv11H DOES hydroxylate β-colubrine alone to form 11-deMe brucine; SnvOMT is only needed for the subsequent methylation to brucine. The note confusingly implies Snv11H needs SnvOMT to work on β-colubrine.

corrections.reason: The paper never describes Snv11H as a rate-limiting step. There is no discussion of rate limitation for this enzyme. This appears to be an unsupported assertion.
